articles.title: Over 40,000 Servers Compromised in Ongoing cPanel Exploitation

articles.summary: Wireshark release 4.6.5 fixes 43 vulnerabilities (38 CVEs) and 35 bugs.

articles.title: Telegram Mini Apps abused for crypto scams, Android malware delivery
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered a large-scale fraud operation that uses Telegram's Mini App feature to run crypto scams, impersonate well-known brands, and distribute Android malware. [...]

articles.title: Critrical cPanel flaw mass-exploited in "Sorry" ransomware attacks
articles.summary: A new disclosed cPanel flaw tracked as CVE-2026-41940 is being mass-exploited to breach websites and encrypt data in "Sorry" ransomware attacks. [...]

articles.summary: Cybersecurity company Trellix has announced that it suffered a breach that enabled unauthorized access to a "portion" of its source code. articles.title: 15-year-old detained over French govt agency data breach
articles.summary: French authorities have detained a 15-year-old suspected of selling data stolen in a cyberattack on France Titres (ANTS), the country's agency for issuing and managing administrative documents. [...]

articles.title: Story retracted
articles.summary: BleepingComputer initially published a story about a new data breach at Instructure. Shortly after publication, we determined that the information was incorrect and primarily based on outdated details from a prior incident. The article has been retracted, and we regret the error. [...]

articles.title: Federal agencies must patch cPanel bug by Sunday, CISA says
articles.summary: Incident responders at Rapid7 said successful exploitation of CVE-2026-41940 “grants an attacker control over the cPanel host system, its configurations and databases, and websites it manages.”

articles.title: Cyber incident responders who carried out ransomware attacks given 4-year sentences
articles.summary: Two cybersecurity incident responders who abused their positions to carry out covert ransomware attacks were sentenced to four years in prison.

articles.title: Criminal IP and Securonix ThreatQ Collaborate to Enhance Threat Intelligence Operations
articles.summary: Raw threat intel isn't enough without real-world context. Criminal IP has partnered with Securonix to integrate exposure-based intelligence into ThreatQ, automating analysis and speeding up investigations. [...]

articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new China-aligned espionage campaign targeting government and defense sectors across South, East, and Southeast Asia, along with one European government belonging to NATO. articles.title: British cyber agency warns of looming ‘patch wave’ as AI speeds flaw discovery
articles.summary: Britain’s cyber agency warned that organizations should prepare for a surge of urgent software updates as artificial intelligence accelerates the discovery of security flaws, raising the risk of widespread exploitation.

articles.title: Cyber spies target Russian aviation firms to steal satellite and GPS data
articles.summary: A cyber-espionage group has been targeting Russian government agencies and companies in the aviation industry to steal sensitive geospatial data.

articles.title: Nearly every Linux system built since 2017 vulnerable to ‘Copy Fail’ flaw
articles.summary: Security researchers and European cybersecurity officials are urging administrators to address the risk posed by a newly discovered security flaw that has been hiding in the Linux operating system for nearly a decade.

articles.title: Hackers earning millions from hijacked cargo, FBI says
articles.summary: In an advisory this week, FBI officials said cyber actors have spent the last two years breaking into the systems of brokers and carriers — allowing them to pose as victim companies and post fraudulent listings on freight delivery message boards.

articles.summary: The U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) warned the transportation and logistics industry of a sharp rise in cyber-enabled cargo theft, with estimated losses in the United States and Canada reaching nearly $725 million in 2025. [...]

articles.title: France investigates 15-year-old over alleged hack of national ID agency
articles.summary: The minor was taken into police custody on April 25 on suspicion of involvement in a data breach affecting the National Agency for Secure Documents (ANTS), which processes applications for passports, national identity cards, residence permits and driver’s licenses.
